question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user who can intercept HTTP traffic between the pinger and mirror REST endpoint return HTTP 301/302 redirects to a slow server, causing httpClient.Do() to follow the redirect and hang for the full mirrorNodeClientTimeout, multiplying delay across retries to exceed 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror node response where all nodeEntry records have ServiceEndpoints with port values of 65535 pointing to hosts that refuse connections, causing the SDK to fail on every gRPC dial attempt and effectively freezing all transaction submissions beyond the 500% block-time threshold?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror node response containing thousands of nodeEntry records with valid-looking but unresponsive endpoints, causing the SDK to attempt connections to all of them sequentially and freezing transaction submissions for a duration exceeding 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the context cancellation path (case <-ctx.Done()) by causing the mirror node to respond slowly enough that the pinger's context times out during the backoff sleep, returning ctx.Err() and permanently preventing network map construction and all transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror node response with HTTP 200 and a valid JSON body where all serviceEndpoint Port values are negative integers (e.g., -1), causing net.JoinHostPort() to produce invalid address strings that the SDK cannot connect to, effectively freezing all transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror node response where all nodeEntry records have GrpcProxyEndpoint set to an unresponsive server while ServiceEndpoints is empty, causing the endpoint selection logic to produce an empty network map and freezing all transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum backoff cap in the mirror node client backoff formula (cfg.mirrorNodeClientBaseBackoff * time.Duration(1<<(attempt-1))) to cause unbounded sleep durations when mirrorNodeClientMaxRetries is large, delaying transaction submissions far beyond the 500% block-time threshold?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror node response with HTTP 200 and a JSON body where the 'nodes' array contains entries with NodeAccountID values that are syntactically valid but semantically invalid for hiero.AccountIDFromString(), causing all nodes to be silently skipped and producing an empty network map?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror node response where all serviceEndpoint DomainName values are valid hostnames that resolve to loopback (127.0.0.1), causing the SDK to attempt gRPC connections to the pinger's own host and fail, effectively freezing all transaction submissions to the real Hedera network?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror REST endpoint to return HTTP 429 on the first attempt and HTTP 500 on subsequent attempts, ensuring the retry condition is always true and the pinger exhausts all retries with exponential backoff, delaying transaction submissions beyond the 500% block-time threshold?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror node response with HTTP 200 and a JSON body where all serviceEndpoint entries have Port values of 1 (a reserved port), causing the SDK to fail on every gRPC connection attempt and freezing all transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror node response where the JSON body is syntactically valid but semantically empty (e.g., '{}'), causing json.Decode to produce a zero-value nodesEnvelope with nil Nodes, resulting in an empty network map and the 'no usable service_endpoints found' error?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() is called synchronously in newClient() (sdk_client.go) to delay the entire pinger startup by causing repeated mirror node failures, preventing any transaction from being submitted for a duration exceeding 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror node response with HTTP 200 and a JSON body containing a 'nodes' array where every entry has ServiceEndpoints with DomainName values that are valid IPv6 addresses in an environment that does not support IPv6, causing net.JoinHostPort() to produce addresses the SDK cannot connect to and freezing all transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror node response where all serviceEndpoint IPAddressV4 values are broadcast addresses (e.g., 255.255.255.255), causing the SDK to fail on every gRPC connection attempt and effectively freezing all transaction submissions beyond the 500% block-time threshold?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of TLS verification in the httpClient (no custom TLS config is set) to perform a man-in-the-middle attack, serving a crafted mirror node response with unresponsive endpoints that causes the SDK to freeze on every transaction submission attempt?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror node response with HTTP 200 and a JSON body where the 'nodes' array contains a single entry with a valid NodeAccountID but ServiceEndpoints pointing to a tarpit server, causing the SDK to hang on every gRPC connection attempt and freezing all transaction submissions beyond the 500% block-time threshold?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror REST endpoint to return HTTP 500 on the last retry attempt (attempt == attempts), triggering the 'break' condition and returning the lastErr, permanently preventing network map construction and freezing all transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror node response with HTTP 200 and a JSON body where all serviceEndpoint DomainName values contain null bytes or control characters, causing net.JoinHostPort() to produce addresses that the SDK's gRPC dialer rejects, resulting in failed transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror node response with HTTP 200 and a JSON body where all serviceEndpoint Port values are 65536 or higher (out of valid TCP range), causing net.JoinHostPort() to produce addresses that the SDK cannot connect to and freezing all transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of response size limits on json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode() to serve an extremely large JSON payload that consumes all available memory in the pinger process, causing an OOM condition that freezes all transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror node response with HTTP 200 and a JSON body containing a deeply nested structure that causes json.Decode to recurse deeply and either stack-overflow or consume excessive CPU, delaying network map construction and all transaction submissions beyond the 500% block-time threshold?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror node response with HTTP 200 and a JSON body where all nodeEntry records have ServiceEndpoints with DomainName values that are extremely long strings (e.g., 64KB each), causing net.JoinHostPort() to produce oversized address strings that the SDK's gRPC dialer rejects, freezing all transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the httpClient has no connection pool limits by causing the mirror REST endpoint to accept TCP connections but never send a response, causing httpClient.Do() to hold open connections until the mirrorNodeClientTimeout expires and delaying all transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror node response with HTTP 200 and a JSON body where all serviceEndpoint entries have IPAddressV4 values of '0.0.0.0', causing the SDK to attempt gRPC connections to the unspecified address and fail, effectively freezing all transaction submissions?
